notes: Revert run -- restored src/analytics/causal_war.py to its pre-190227Z state for ONLY the venue+fielding-alignment changes that caused regression (combined Spearman 0.631 -> 0.494, gate FAIL). Specifically reverted: (1) LEFT JOIN games g restored in _extract_pa_data, removed home_team/away_team/if_fielding_alignment/of_fielding_alignment columns from the pitches CTE; (2) venue branch in _build_features restored to dead-feature behavior (only label-encodes if df['venue'] populated, which it is not under the games-table LEFT JOIN since games is empty); (3) if_shift = 0 and of_shift = 0 hardcoded restored. PRESERVED: the morning agent's pitcher-cohort fix in train_test_split() (lines ~335-435 of the file) -- per-pitcher DML residual aggregation with -Y_res by pitcher_id and pa_min=50 floor, plus the corresponding test_pitcher_effects/n_test_pitchers/test_pitcher_player_effects additions to test_metrics and the returned dict. scripts/baseline_comparison.py was untouched (already correctly merges both cohorts via morning fix). Post-revert numbers exactly match 171420Z baseline (0.0000 delta on all four headline metrics). Model artifact at models/causal_war/causal_war_trainsplit_2015_2022.pkl now contains the restored model. Venue/fielding-alignment 'fixes' are deferred research items (see appended verdict in docs/models/causal_war_baseline_results.md), not abandoned -- the regression appears driven by 2023 shift-ban distribution shift on fielding features and team-park-era nontransferability on home_team 30-level encoding, both of which require more sophisticated treatment (target encoding, era-stratified splits) than a one-line factor() swap.
